title: OpenAI Ships GPT-5 with Persistent Memory and Tool Use at Scale
summary: OpenAI released GPT-5 this week, featuring native long-context memory that persists across conversations and significantly improved tool-calling reliability. The model is available in the API with a new tiered pricing structure.
officerTake: This is the real leap we were waiting for. Persistent memory changes the entire product surface — now you can build assistants that actually remember your preferences without you managing vector stores manually. The tool-use reliability bump is also huge for agentic workflows. Expect a workshop on this next meeting.

title: AlphaFold 3 Predicts Protein-Drug Interactions with 91% Accuracy
summary: DeepMind's AlphaFold 3 extends beyond protein structure prediction to model how small drug molecules interact with proteins. In a benchmark of 2,000 known drug-target pairs, it matched experimental results 91% of the time.
officerTake: AlphaFold keeps raising the ceiling for what ML can do in science. The move from structure prediction to interaction modeling is the important shift here — this is the kind of result that makes pharma pipelines actually usable. Highly recommend reading the Nature paper even if bio isn't your thing; the architecture innovations are transferable.

title: NVIDIA's Blackwell Ultra GPUs Ship to Hyperscalers — 2x the Memory Bandwidth
summary: NVIDIA began shipping Blackwell Ultra B300 GPUs to major cloud providers this week, doubling HBM4 memory bandwidth over the original Blackwell. The chips are targeted at massive MoE models that were previously memory-bound.
officerTake: More bandwidth means bigger models run cheaper at inference. This eventually trickles down to API pricing, which means we can do more complex projects without burning through free credits. The MoE angle is interesting — I'd expect to see a lot more research released on sparse expert routing in the next few months.

title: Anthropic Releases Claude 4 Family with 32K Token Thinking Budget
summary: Anthropic released the Claude 4 model family, including Claude Opus 4 and Sonnet 4, featuring extended thinking with up to 32,000 tokens of internal reasoning. Early benchmarks show significant gains on multi-step math and coding tasks.
officerTake: Extended thinking is what makes Claude genuinely dangerous on hard problems. 32k thinking tokens is a lot of room to reason through edge cases before giving an answer. For our EssayCoach project, this could mean much better structured feedback without us needing to do as much prompt engineering.

title: OpenAI's Sora 2 Generates Coherent 5-Minute Videos from Text
summary: Sora 2 was released publicly with the ability to generate videos up to 5 minutes long with maintained scene coherence. The model also accepts image + text inputs for story continuation.
officerTake: 5 minutes of coherent video is genuinely impressive — that's past the threshold for short-form content. The interesting ML challenge here isn't the generation itself, it's temporal consistency over that length. Worth studying how they extended the diffusion architecture.

title: Tesla FSD v13 Achieves 'Supervised Autonomy' on Texas Highways
summary: Tesla's FSD v13, trained almost entirely on video data without LiDAR, achieved supervised autonomy classification in Texas — meaning it can handle most driving scenarios without human intervention under favorable conditions.
officerTake: This matters for the computer vision community because Tesla is doing this with cameras only. Pure vision-based autonomy is what a lot of researchers said couldn't work at scale. Whether it's actually safe at deployment is a separate question — but the technical achievement is real.
